verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein with a single transmembrane domain localized to both the Golgi cisternae and plasma membrane. The NCBI summary explicitly states 'localized to the plasma membrane and golgi cisternae.' As a member of the p24 family involved in vesicular trafficking, it cycles between these compartments. The dominant compartment is the secretory pathway (Golgi), but the protein has a documented PM pool via its own TM anchor as part of normal trafficking dynamics. This dual localization qualifies for contextual surface accessibility via the dual_localization mechanism.
